text: 68-year-old male admitted with STEMI, LAD occlusion. Primary PCI performed within 90 minutes. Drug-eluting stent placed. EF post-procedure 42%. Aspirin + ticagrelor initiated.
Patient with chronic heart failure, NYHA Class III, EF 30%. BNP 1840 pg/mL. Optimized on carvedilol, lisinopril, spironolactone. ICD referral discussed.
55-year-old female with paroxysmal atrial fibrillation. CHA2DS2-VASc score 3. Initiated rivaroxaban 20mg daily. Rate control with metoprolol 50mg BID.
Hypertensive urgency, BP 198/112 on arrival. No end-organ damage. IV labetalol administered, transitioned to oral amlodipine. Discharged with medication reconciliation.
Stable angina pectoris, CCS Class II. Stress nuclear imaging shows fixed inferior wall defect. Coronary angiography planned. Isosorbide mononitrate 30mg added.
Complete heart block in 77-year-old with syncope. Temporary transvenous pacing initiated. Permanent dual-chamber pacemaker implanted without complications.
Hypertrophic obstructive cardiomyopathy confirmed on echocardiogram. Septal thickness 22mm. Gradient 56mmHg. Beta-blocker titrated. Genetic counseling referral placed.
Acute decompensated heart failure. BNP 3200. IV furosemide diuresis initiated. Daily weights, strict fluid restriction. Renal function monitored.
Patient with peripheral artery disease, ABI 0.62 bilateral. Claudication limiting ambulation to one block. Supervised exercise therapy and cilostazol initiated.
Pericarditis confirmed by diffuse ST elevation and PR depression on ECG. Echo shows small effusion. High-dose aspirin + colchicine prescribed. Strenuous activity restricted 3 months.
